summary.intent: Build a polished Electron app UI for gossamer using Mantine, with a GitHub-style dark/light mode, chat transcript layout for session details, and the ability to spawn new Claude Code sessions from open items in the overview panel.
summary.outcome: Fully migrated all screens/components to Mantine, implemented dark mode with light-dark() CSS throughout, redesigned session detail as a chat transcript with avatars, added relative timestamps, breadcrumb navigation, and a working 'spawn session' feature that opens a new Terminal window running claude with the prompt pre-filled.

repo: Gossamer is an Electron + electron-vite + React + React Router (MemoryRouter) app at /Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer/app/
Backend is a Node.js HTTP server at /Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer/src/server.ts using Prisma/SQLite, with session/checkpoint data fed by claude-hook-handler
Hook handler binary is at /opt/homebrew/bin/claude-hook-handler and writes to ~/.claude/hook-handler.db; hooks configured in ~/.claude/settings.json
Claude Code SDK (query()) calls the Anthropic API directly — it does NOT invoke the claude CLI, so gossamer hooks never fire when using query() without settingSources: ['user', 'project']
The CLAUDECODE env var blocks nested claude CLI invocations; must be deleted from process.env before spawning via SDK or CLI
settingSources: ['user', 'project'] in query() options causes the SDK to load ~/.claude/settings.json hooks, allowing gossamer to capture SDK-spawned sessions
CheckpointSessionMetadata.createdAt is the source for checkpoint timestamps; CheckpointMetadata has no createdAt field
Activity status thresholds: <2min = green (active), <15min = orange (idle), else gray (inactive), based on session.updatedAt

code.finding: Had entirely hardcoded hex colors for all text, code blocks, and tables — none adapted to dark mode. All replaced with light-dark() equivalents.
groupClaudeTurns() merges consecutive toolGroup DisplayItems + following Stop event into a single claudeTurn RenderItem for chat transcript layout
New shared component: shows relative time, tooltip with absolute on hover, click-to-copy. Also exports relativeTime() and absoluteTime() helpers used in non-tooltip contexts.
React context for pushing breadcrumb state from child pages up to Layout header, avoiding prop drilling through router outlet
TypeScript declaration file needed for PNG imports: declare module '*.png' { const src: string; export default src; }

POST /api/sessions/spawn uses execSync('osascript') to open a new Terminal window running claude with the prompt. The query() approach was tried but abandoned because it doesn't fire gossamer hooks without settingSources, and interactive use required the CLI anyway.

workflow: light-dark(lightValue, darkValue) CSS function is the correct pattern for theme-aware colors in Mantine v7 — Mantine's X-0 color variants (e.g. gray-0, teal-0) are always the lightest shade regardless of color scheme
AppShell.Header spans the full viewport width above both navbar and main content — this is the correct place for elements that must always be visible regardless of sidebar state
Claude Code SDK query() calls Anthropic API directly, not the claude CLI; this is a critical distinction for hook-based observability systems
osascript + AppleScript is a simple macOS-specific solution for opening new Terminal windows; acceptable for now but noted as platform-specific
Claude logo can be extracted from /Applications/Claude.app/Contents/Resources/electron.icns using: iconutil -c iconset electron.icns -o claude.iconset

friction: Two context window exhaustions during a single session required summary-based continuation, losing some exact code state
Dark mode required fixing hardcoded colors in 8+ files — the original migration to Mantine used X-0 color variants which look correct in light mode but are always light-colored
CLAUDECODE env var silently blocks SDK spawning with no clear error message — spent significant time debugging before identifying the cause
SDK query() events look successful (system/assistant/result) but don't fire gossamer hooks unless settingSources: ['user', 'project'] is specified — this is not obvious from SDK docs
AppleScript spawn approach is macOS-only, conflicting with the stated goal of platform-agnostic tooling
Server restart required manually after each backend change — no hot reload for the backend
open_items: Platform-agnostic session spawning: the current osascript/AppleScript approach only works on macOS; needs a cross-platform solution (xterm, xdg-open, Windows Terminal, etc.)
CheckpointTimeline.tsx and CheckpointDetail.tsx were rewritten for Mantine but not audited for dark mode color issues in this session
The overview panel intent/open-items come from the most recent checkpoint summary — no handling for the case where the checkpoint summary is still processing or malformed
SessionDetail breadcrumb sets user/repo from session data but doesn't update if the session data changes (e.g. first load vs. full load race)
No error handling shown to user if spawnSession API call fails (server-side osascript error)
The spawn feature passes parentSessionId in the query but no mechanism verifies the child session actually references the parent in gossamer's data model
